intro:
There is no information available on the plant Hylodesmum repandum. 

However, Hylodesmum repandum is a plant species, but I found information about the plant Hylodesmum repandum seems to be a misnomer for the plant Hylodesmum caeruleum (Blue-pea or butterfly pea) in some sources. 

Hylodesmum caeruleum, also known as Hylodesmum repandum, is a flowering plant species in the pea family, Fabaceae. 

It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.

The bioactive constituents of Hylodesmum caeruleum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins.

Hylodesmum caeruleum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are believed to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and improve cognitive function.
